instructions: Answer the following questions to test your knowledge before starting the experiment.

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of database normalization?
options: To increase database size
To eliminate data redundancy and reduce anomalies
To make queries faster
To add more tables

questions.question: What is a functional dependency?
options: A relationship between two tables
A constraint where one set of attributes determines another set of attributes
A foreign key relationship
A primary key constraint

questions.question: What does 1NF (First Normal Form) require?
options: All attributes must contain atomic (indivisible) values
No partial dependencies
No transitive dependencies
All determinants must be candidate keys

questions.question: What is a partial dependency?
options: A dependency where Y depends on X
A dependency where a non-key attribute depends on part of a composite key
A dependency where Y depends on a non-key attribute
A transitive dependency

questions.question: What does 2NF (Second Normal Form) require?
options: The relation must be in 1NF only
The relation must be in 1NF and have no partial dependencies
The relation must have no transitive dependencies
All determinants must be candidate keys

questions.question: What is a transitive dependency?
options: A → B and B → C, so A → C
A non-key attribute depends on another non-key attribute, which depends on the primary key
A partial dependency on a composite key
A dependency where X → Y and Y → X

questions.question: What does 3NF (Third Normal Form) require?
options: The relation must be in 1NF only
The relation must be in 2NF only
The relation must be in 2NF and have no transitive dependencies
All determinants must be candidate keys

questions.question: What is an insertion anomaly?
options: Cannot delete data without losing other information
Cannot insert certain information without other information
Updating one value requires updating multiple rows
Data redundancy in the database

questions.question: What is a candidate key?
options: Any set of attributes that uniquely identifies a tuple
A minimal superkey (no proper subset is a superkey)
A foreign key in another table
A composite key

questions.question: What is the difference between BCNF and 3NF?
options: There is no difference
BCNF requires that every determinant is a candidate key, while 3NF allows non-key determinants
BCNF is less strict than 3NF
3NF eliminates all redundancy, BCNF does not

questions.question: What is a deletion anomaly?
options: Deleting one piece of information causes loss of other information
Cannot insert data without other information
Updating one value requires updating multiple rows
Data is stored redundantly

questions.question: In the relation R(A, B, C) with functional dependencies A → B and B → C, what normal form is it in?
options: 1NF
2NF
2NF (but not 3NF due to transitive dependency)
3NF

questions.question: What is a superkey?
options: A set of attributes that uniquely identifies a tuple
A minimal set of attributes that uniquely identifies a tuple
A foreign key
A primary key

questions.question: What is an update anomaly?
options: Cannot insert data without other information
Deleting data causes loss of other information
Changing one value requires updating multiple rows
Data redundancy

questions.question: What is the goal of normalization?
options: To make queries faster
To eliminate redundancy and reduce anomalies while maintaining data integrity
To increase database size
To add more tables
